task_completion.description: Open the target image in GIMP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GIMP is open and displays the original image (either snowy mountains, ocean waves, or a synthetic blue rectangle) in the main workspace.

task_completion.description: Initiate the process of creating a border.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A border-related tool or dialog is active. Look for a 'Border Selection' dialog, an 'Add Border' filter dialog, or a selection marquee (marching ants) outlining the inner edges of the image.

task_completion.description: Apply a uniform border to the image edges.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image canvas in GIMP shows a distinct, uniform dark border (approximately 15-30 pixels wide) along all four edges. The center of the image must remain clearly visible and unchanged.

task_completion.description: Open the export dialog to save the modified image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Export Image' dialog box is visible in GIMP.

task_completion.description: Export the image with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filename in the export dialog is set to 'bordered_image.jpg' (or a similar standard image extension) and the agent is confirming the export.

integrity.description: The agent must use the GIMP graphical user interface to edit the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Image editing steps and export should occur within the GIMP GUI. The agent should not use terminal commands (like ImageMagick's 'convert') to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The agent must actually modify the image in GIMP.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the progression of adding the border (e.g., active selections, tool usage, or layer changes), rather than just opening a pre-existing bordered image.

integrity.description: The center of the image must not be obscured or altered.
integrity.visual_evidence: The border must only affect the outer edges (up to ~30 pixels). The central area of the image must remain completely intact and free of any drawing, filling, or darkening.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires adding a border to an image using GIMP 2.10. The original image will be a snowy mountain, ocean waves, or a synthetic blue rectangle. The border should be uniform, applied to all four edges, approximately 15-30 pixels wide, and significantly darken the edges (>=15% darkening). The center of the image (excluding a 50-pixel margin) must remain >95% unchanged. The final image must be exported from GIMP as 'bordered_image.jpg'.
